name: Return Policy
text: The Buyer may return Goods to the Supplier in the following circumstances: (a) the Goods are defective or damaged upon delivery; (b) the Goods do not conform to agreed specifications; (c) the Supplier has delivered incorrect Goods; or (d) the Goods are subject to a recall by the Supplier or a competent regulatory authority. All returns must be initiated within thirty (30) days of delivery by written notice specifying the reason for return. Returned Goods must be in their original packaging wherever practicable. The Supplier shall bear all costs of return logistics for returns arising under (a) through (d) above. Upon receipt of validly returned Goods, the Supplier shall, within fourteen (14) days, at the Buyer's option, either replace the Goods or refund the purchase price, together with any applicable GST.

legal_basis.note: Consumer rights — applicable to consumer transactions

invalid_if: Return window less than 7 days for consumer goods
Costs of return for defective goods placed on Buyer
title: Return Policy
dispute_hotspots: Consumer Protection Act — mandatory return window for consumer goods cannot be contractually excluded
Return shipping cost — seller liability for defective goods, buyer for change of mind
Credit note vs refund — B2B returns typically credit note; B2C may require cash refund
